response.justification: The candidate is a strong senior full-stack/web engineer with nearly two decades of software experience, including some systems-level work (low-latency DLang services, C/C++ at National Instruments). However, the role requires deep hardware-adjacent expertise — SoC/IP modeling, performance analysis of deep-learning workloads, GPU architecture, and hardware-software co-design — none of which appear in the resume. The career trajectory centers on web platforms, product engineering, and developer tooling rather than performance architecture for AI accelerators, making this a significant domain mismatch despite the candidate's seniority.
strengths: 18+ years of software engineering experience, exceeding the years-of-experience bar for a Bachelor's-degree path
Low-latency, high-throughput systems experience (millions of HTTP requests/sec, async I/O, fibers, caching in DLang)
Strong engineering craftsmanship: TDD, CI/CD, open-source contributions, and technical community leadership
concerns: No SoC/IP modeling, silicon, or hardware architecture experience whatsoever
No deep-learning training/inference optimization or GPU/SIMD/MIMD workload analysis background
Recent experience is web/product-focused (TypeScript, React, MongoDB), far from principal-level performance architecture

key_factors.factor: No AI accelerator or DL workload optimization experience
